batch

Class BatchBuilder

  • java.lang.Object
    • batch.BatchBuilder

  • public final class BatchBuilder
extends java.lang.Object

    Implements the Builder design pattern for the construction and validation of MediaBatchProcessor configurations.

    This class serves as the central gateway for defining batch parameters. It can produce a self-initiating MediaMetadataConsole via build() for CLI applications, or an immutable BatchConfiguration via buildConfig() for custom implementations such as GUI.

    Example (CLI): 

     
 BatchConsole batch = new BatchBuilder()
 .source("D:\\Media\\Photos")
 .target("local\\images")
 .prefix("holiday")
 .descending(true)
 .userDate("26 04 2006")
 .build();

    Example (GUI): 

     
 BatchConfiguration config = new BatchBuilder()
 .source(txtField.getText())
 .buildConfig();
    Since:
    24 April 2026
    Version:
    1.0
    Author:
    Trevor Maggs

      Modifier and Type Field Description

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      MediaMetadataConsole build()
      Validates configuration constraints and instantiates the console application.
      BatchConfiguration buildConfig()
      Validates the current configuration and returns an immutable BatchConfiguration snapshot.
      BatchBuilder debug(boolean b)
      Enables or disables debug mode for more verbose logging.
      BatchBuilder descending(boolean b)
      Configures the sorting order of the processed images to be descending.
      BatchBuilder embedDateTime(boolean b)
      Determines whether the date and time attribute should be prepended to each output file name.
      BatchBuilder fileSet(java.lang.String[] f)
      Specifies a defined set of individual files to copy, rather than processing the entire source directory.
      BatchBuilder forceDateChange(boolean b)
      Forces the system to prioritise the user-defined date, regardless of any existing metadata present in the file.
      BatchBuilder prefix(java.lang.String p)
      Sets a prefix to be prepended uniformly to each output file name.
      BatchBuilder showMetadata(boolean b)
      Configures the builder to display a list of metadata entries for the processed files.
      BatchBuilder skipVideo(boolean b)
      Defines whether video files should be skipped or included in the batch.
      BatchBuilder source(java.lang.String s)
      Sets the source directory containing the original media files.
      BatchBuilder target(java.lang.String t)
      Sets the target directory where the processed files will be saved.
      BatchBuilder userDate(java.lang.String d)
      Sets a specific date used to override both the EXIF Date Taken metadata and the file system modification timestamp.
      private void validate()
      Validates the builder's state against internal constraint rules to maintain configuration integrity.

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

        public BatchBuilder()

    • Method Detail

      • source

        public BatchBuilder source(java.lang.String s)
        Sets the source directory containing the original media files.
        Parameters:
        s - the source directory
        Returns:
        this builder instance to allow method chaining

      • target

        public BatchBuilder target(java.lang.String t)
        Sets the target directory where the processed files will be saved.
        Parameters:
        t - the target directory
        Returns:
        this builder instance to allow method chaining

      • prefix

        public BatchBuilder prefix(java.lang.String p)
        Sets a prefix to be prepended uniformly to each output file name.
        Parameters:
        p - the string to be prepended to every copied file
        Returns:
        this builder instance to allow method chaining

      • userDate

        public BatchBuilder userDate(java.lang.String d)
        Sets a specific date used to override both the EXIF Date Taken metadata and the file system modification timestamp.
        Parameters:
        d - the date and time string to be used for processing
        Returns:
        this builder instance to allow method chaining

      • fileSet

        public BatchBuilder fileSet(java.lang.String[] f)
        Specifies a defined set of individual files to copy, rather than processing the entire source directory.
        Parameters:
        f - a string array of specific filenames
        Returns:
        this builder instance to allow method chaining

      • forceDateChange

        public BatchBuilder forceDateChange(boolean b)
        Forces the system to prioritise the user-defined date, regardless of any existing metadata present in the file.
        Parameters:
        b - true to enable forced user date/time in the filename
        Returns:
        this builder instance to allow method chaining

      • embedDateTime

        public BatchBuilder embedDateTime(boolean b)
        Determines whether the date and time attribute should be prepended to each output file name.
        Parameters:
        b - true to include the date/time in the filename, or false to omit it
        Returns:
        this builder instance to allow method chaining

      • skipVideo

        public BatchBuilder skipVideo(boolean b)
        Defines whether video files should be skipped or included in the batch.
        Parameters:
        b - true to skip video files, otherwise copy them
        Returns:
        this builder instance to allow method chaining

      • showMetadata

        public BatchBuilder showMetadata(boolean b)
        Configures the builder to display a list of metadata entries for the processed files.
        Parameters:
        b - true to enable metadata display
        Returns:
        this builder instance to allow method chaining

      • descending

        public BatchBuilder descending(boolean b)
        Configures the sorting order of the processed images to be descending.
        Parameters:
        b - true to sort in descending order, otherwise ascending
        Returns:
        this builder instance to allow method chaining

      • debug

        public BatchBuilder debug(boolean b)
        Enables or disables debug mode for more verbose logging.
        Parameters:
        b - true to enable debugging
        Returns:
        this builder instance to allow method chaining

      • build

        public MediaMetadataConsole build()
        Validates configuration constraints and instantiates the console application.
        Returns:
        a new MediaMetadataConsole instance

      • buildConfig

        public BatchConfiguration buildConfig()
        Validates the current configuration and returns an immutable BatchConfiguration snapshot.
        Returns:
        a validated, immutable configuration object
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if validation rules are violated

      • validate

        private void validate()
        Validates the builder's state against internal constraint rules to maintain configuration integrity.

        This validation ensures that:

        • A user-defined date is present when the force flag is enabled.
        • A source directory has been explicitly specified.
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if the force flag is enabled without a user date, or if the source directory is missing
